Pixel Neural Networks
  • Pixel-Neural-Networks
  • architecture module
  • View page source

architecture module

class architecture.Encoder(in_channels: int = 3, latent_dim: int = 128)[source]

Bases: Module

Convolutional encoder mapping an image to a latent vector.

Used as the conditioning encoder in PixelCNNAutoencoder.

Parameters:

  • in_channels (int) – Number of input image channels.

  • latent_dim (int) – Dimensionality of the output latent vector.

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Encode an image batch to latent vectors.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input images of shape (B, C, H, W).

Returns:

Latent vectors of shape (B, latent_dim).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.FinalBlock(n_channel: int = 3, h: int = 128, discrete_channel: int = 256)[source]

Bases: Module

Final PixelCNN block projecting features to output logits.

Projects the feature map to n_channel * discrete_channel channels using two 1x1 convolutions with ReLU and BatchNorm in between.

Parameters:

  • n_channel (int) – Number of image channels.

  • h (int) – Bottleneck dimension.

  • discrete_channel (int) – Number of discrete values per channel (default 256).

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Forward pass through the final projection block.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, 2h, H, W).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, n_channel * discrete_channel, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.FirstBlock(c_in: int = 3, c_out: int = 256, kernel_size: int = 7, stride: int = 1, pad: int = 3)[source]

Bases: Module

First PixelCNN block using type-A masked convolution.

Applies a 7x7 type-A MaskedConv followed by BatchNorm. Used as the entry point of the network to ensure no pixel attends to itself or future pixels.

Parameters:

  • c_in (int) – Number of input channels.

  • c_out (int) – Number of output channels.

  • kernel_size (int) – Kernel size (default 7).

  • stride (int) – Stride (default 1).

  • pad (int) – Padding (default 3).

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Forward pass through the first block.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, C_in, H, W).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, C_out, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.GatedActivation(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)[source]

Bases: Module

Gated activation: tanh(a) * sigmoid(b).

Splits channels in half along dim=1, applies tanh to the first half and sigmoid to the second half, then multiplies element-wise.

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Apply gated activation to input tensor.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, 2C, H, W).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, C, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.GatedPixelCNNBlock(channels: int)[source]

Bases: Module

One layer of a Gated PixelCNN combining vertical and horizontal stacks.

Processes the vertical and horizontal feature maps jointly, accumulates a skip connection output, and returns updated streams.

Parameters:

channels (int) – Number of feature channels (same for input and output).

forward(v: Tensor, h: Tensor, skip: Tensor) → tuple[source]

Run one gated block and update the skip accumulator.

Parameters:

  • v (torch.Tensor) – Vertical input of shape (B, C, H, W).

  • h (torch.Tensor) – Horizontal input of shape (B, C, H, W).

  • skip (torch.Tensor) – Accumulated skip tensor of shape (B, C, H, W).

Returns:

Updated (v_out, h_out, skip), each of shape (B, C, H, W).

Return type:

tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]

class architecture.HorizontalStack(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: int = 3)[source]

Bases: Module

Causal horizontal convolution: pads (kernel_size-1) columns on the left. Fuses the vertical-stack output before gating.

Parameters:

  • in_channels (int) – Number of input channels.

  • out_channels (int) – Number of output channels.

  • kernel_size (int) – Spatial kernel size (default 3).

forward(h: Tensor, v: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Apply causal left-padding, fuse vertical output, gate, and add residual.

Parameters:

  • h (torch.Tensor) – Horizontal input feature map of shape (B, C, H, W).

  • v (torch.Tensor) – Vertical feature map from VerticalStack of shape (B, C, H, W).

Returns:

Output feature map of shape (B, out_channels, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.MaskedConv(mask_type: str, c_in: int, c_out: int, kernel_size: int, stride: int, pad: int)[source]

Bases: Conv2d

2D convolution with causal masking for PixelCNN.

Implements two mask types:

  • Type ‘A’: excludes the center pixel and all subsequent pixels in raster scan order. Used in the first layer to ensure predictions do not depend on themselves.

  • Type ‘B’: includes the center pixel but excludes subsequent ones. Used in residual layers to allow self-connection.

Parameters:

  • mask_type (str) – ‘A’ or ‘B’.

  • c_in (int) – Number of input channels.

  • c_out (int) – Number of output channels.

  • kernel_size (int) – Kernel size.

  • stride (int) – Stride.

  • pad (int) – Padding.

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Apply masked convolution to input tensor.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, C_in, H, W).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, C_out, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.ResidualBlock(h: int = 128, kernel_size: int = 3, stride: int = 1, pad: int = 1)[source]

Bases: Module

Residual PixelCNN block with bottleneck pattern.

Architecture:

2h -> h (1x1) -> h (3x3 masked B) -> 2h (1x1)

Includes residual skip connection.

Parameters:

  • h (int) – Bottleneck dimension.

  • kernel_size (int) – Kernel size for the masked convolution (default 3).

  • stride (int) – Stride (default 1).

  • pad (int) – Padding (default 1).

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Forward pass through the residual block with skip connection.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, 2h, H, W).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, 2h, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.ResidualRowLSTMBlock(in_out_channels: int)[source]

Bases: Module

Residual Row-LSTM block with 1x1 projection.

Applies a RowLSTM with internal bottleneck (in_out_channels // 2) followed by a 1x1 convolution to restore the channel dimension, and adds a residual skip connection.

Parameters:

in_out_channels (int) – Number of input and output channels.

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Forward pass with residual connection.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, C, H, W).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, C, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.RowLSTM(in_channels: int, hidden_channels: int, kernel_width: int = 3)[source]

Bases: Module

Row-LSTM: processes one row at a time top-to-bottom, maintaining causal context (no future rows visible).

Uses a (1, kernel_width) input conv + 1x1 hidden-to-hidden conv.

Parameters:

  • in_channels (int) – Number of input channels.

  • hidden_channels (int) – Number of hidden/output channels.

  • kernel_width (int) – Width of the input-to-hidden convolution kernel (default 3).

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Forward pass of Row-LSTM over all rows.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, C, H, W).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, hidden_channels, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor

class architecture.VerticalStack(in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: int = 3)[source]

Bases: Module

Causal vertical convolution stack for GatedPixelCNN.

Pads (kernel_size - 1) rows on the top only so the convolution cannot attend to any row below the current position.

Parameters:

  • in_channels (int) – Number of input channels.

  • out_channels (int) – Number of output channels (before gating; the conv produces 2 * out_channels which GatedActivation halves).

  • kernel_size (int) – Spatial kernel size (default 3).

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Apply causal top-padding, convolution, and gated activation.

Parameters:

x (torch.Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (B, C, H, W).

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (B, out_channels, H, W).

Return type:

torch.Tensor
